name: Will Marion Cook
abstract: William Mercer Cook (January 27, 1869 – July 19, 1944), better known as Will Marion Cook, was an African-American composer, pianist, orchestrator, lyricist, violinist, and choral director. Cook was a student of Antonín Dvořák.

concerts.label: Benefit: World's Fair Colored Opera Company
Clef Club Orchestra: Concert of Negro Music
Clef Club Orchestra: Concert of Negro Music

works_created.t: If the sands of the sea were peerless pearls

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Wilber
about_quotes.evidence: He went to France with Will Marion Cook’s Southern Syncopated Orchestra in 1919
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Wilber interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 22, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Wooding
about_quotes.evidence: Will Marion Cook used to come there because that was his wife... I could then pick up the tempo... I just wasn't afraid to go over to Old Man Cook, and I said, 'Listen Daddy Cook, Daddy,' I said, 'how do you retard a singer?'

about_quotes.speaker: Danny Barker
about_quotes.evidence: Steele was pointing out everybody to me. “Who’s that?” “That’s Will Marion Cook. That’s . . .” Big names, he be calling the people. I didn’t know who they are, but I know they’re carrying some weight.
about_quotes.doc_title: Danny Barker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Billy Taylor
about_quotes.evidence: She told us many stories like that of people like Paul Robson and Hayes, and earlier people, Will Marion Cook and people who had done things.
about_quotes.doc_title: Billy Taylor — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Hinton Milt
about_quotes.evidence: Will Marion Cook was, and Huey Blake, they were well known. ... Will Marion Cooke’s music was being played but ... He was a great violinist.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hinton Milt — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Wilber
about_quotes.evidence: I'm kind of interested in looking up this music and seeing what it is. You know, I don't know too much about his music.
